
;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;
;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;
;;; A classical example of dependent types
;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;
;;; Simple type definitions:
;;; Defines the natural numbers `Nat` for counting
  (: Nat Type)
  (: Z Nat)
  (: S 
    (-> Nat Nat))
;;; `Vec` takes a `$t` type and a `Nat` length.
  (: Vec 
    (-> $t Nat Type))
;;; `Cons` and `Nil` construct `Vec` values, with `Cons` incrementing the length by 1.
  (: Cons 
    (-> $t 
      (Vec $t $x) 
      (Vec $t 
        (S $x))))
  (: Nil 
    (Vec $t Z))
;;; We can query the derived type
  !(assertEqual 
    (get-type (Cons 0 (Cons 1 Nil))) 
    (Vec Number 
      (S (S Z))))

[

;
;; HYPERON-EXPERIMENTAL-SCRIPTS.D3-DEPTYPES.01

; 
; EVAL TEST
; took 0.001 secs. (1.41 milliseconds) 

  !(assertEqual 
    (get-type (Cons 0 (Cons 1 Nil))) 
    (Vec Number 
      (S (S Z))))

  (loonit_success  
    (equal_enough_for_test  
      ( (Vec Number 
          (S (S Z)))) 
      ( (Vec Number 
          (S (S Z))))))


  (= (error  (existence_error  procedure (/  trace_on_pass 0)) (context  (:  system (/   1)) $_5850))  
    (,  
      (eval_H  500 &self 
        (assertEqual 
          (get-type (Cons 0 (Cons 1 Nil))) 
          (Vec Number 
            (S (S Z)))) $_3564) 
      (,  
        (deterministic  $_3996) 
        (,  
          (xform_out  $_3564 $_4024) 
          (nb_setarg  1 
            (res  $_3320) $_4024)))))




  (= (error  (existence_error  procedure (/  trace_on_pass 0)) (context  (:  system (/   1)) $_5850))

        (assertEqual 
          (get-type (Cons 0 (Cons 1 Nil))) 
          (Vec Number 
            (S (S Z)))) $_3564) 
      (,

        (,

          (nb_setarg  1 
            (res  $_3320) $_4024)))))
